review_text: I love the sound quality. The bass comes across very clear as if you’re in a room listening to a high-quality stereo. The noise cancellation is the best that I’ve experienced. You have the option of either blocking everything out or allowing some noise to come in so that you can be aware in your environment. To give you an idea of how good the noise cancellation is, I have driven with my window down and have been sitting by a couple of Harleys at a stop light, neither instance was too loud for this setting. However, when the Harleys accelerated, I definitely heard them but they were not overwhelmingly loud. Like I mentioned in the title however, the only issue I have with them is they fall out easily no matter what size ear tip I use. So this caused another purchase for me to buy ear hooks to secure them in my ear. I would not buy the over the ear ear hooks however if you’re looking for some get the ones that secure them inside your ear. (see picture)

review_text: This product is almost perfect and probably could be if they fixed two issues. The first, and main, issue I faced was the microphone quality. I was extremely disappointed in the quality. People have repeatedly commented that I am very scratchy on calls and I’ve recorded myself multiple times on Voice Memos using these and the quality was subpar. They actually had worse quality than my iPhone’s built-in microphone and even the Lightning Connector Earpods that came with my phone. I’ve tried troubleshooting this multiple ways including, forgetting the device completely and pairing it again (exactly according to instructions in box) and turning my phone completely off and on again to no avail. I also found that the volume level was somewhat lower than I expected. It isn’t extremely quiet, but to me it just seems a bit low. Now, I don’t know if I am just unlucky and I’ve received a dud or if my expectations for this product are unreasonable, but I’d just like to see these problems resolved if not for my sake, then for the sake of others. Other than those issues, I love the noise cancellation and the battery life is phenomenal.

review_text: The Apple AirPods Pro are a definite step up from the regular AirPods, stay in your ears well (the Beats Fit Pro does slightly better here, but the microphone is not as good as on the AirPods Pro), have good noise cancellation (Bose and Sony might be a tad better, but these are very good), and excellent microphone pickup. If you are going to be in a loot of phone calls, eMeetings (Zoom, Webex, Facetime, etc.) these are very much the ones to get. About the only thing these are not good for would be high head movement activities (athletics), there the Beats Fit Pro does better, but these are on a par or better than the competitive Sony or Bose variants (which are larger, so easier to dislodge). Also, once paired with Apple devices, you can just move them around to be used with whatever you happen to be doing, iPad or iPhone or MacBook, they autoswitch, don't have to be re-paired, and also handle Siri commands.

review_text: So, first off…LOVE! I have all the other Apple Products and these are absolutely amazing and make me soooooo happy! I use them every single day…at the gym, at work, at home, at the store…all day! 95% of the time I only wear one at a time but that’s one of the things I love most because I can do every single thing on the left one or on the right one. They preform identically and it’s amazing. The sound quality is absolutely incredible and there is just so much more. The only problem is my right one physically cuts my ear. Apple is working on this issue with me to figure out what is going on. I’ve sent all kinds of pictures in for them to review. They believe it’s a micro defect on the casing that is basically invisible to the naked eye but is off just enough to cause this issue of cutting my ear. My left one is absolutely perfect and I have zero issue with it at all. I would absolutely recommend these to anyone that is thinking of getting them. I’m sure glad I took the leap on these for sure.

review_text: They do get a little sore in my ears after a while. I wish the battery lasted just a smidge longer. I generally use them while doing things around the house, and they will last a few hours. The sound quality is very good. The input quality is better than the standard corded ear buds. It picks up my voice better. Noise cancellation is comparable to the standard ear buds. My biggest complaint is that these were not clean when I received them. I opted for the open box to get a discount on these. The description indicated that the box may have been opened and possibly damaged. However, it did not indicate that the item had been previously used. And especially considering the current pandemic situation, particular care should have been taken to ensure that the item had been cleaned. I spent about 20 minutes scraping ear wax out of the ear buds! Gross! And if you have had any Apple ear bud before, you know that it is very difficult to get into all of those tiny little crevices! I sincerely wish that Amazon had taken a little more care in the processing of returns such as this!

review_text: This is an absolutely crucial purchase if you work in a shared space (crowded coffee shop), fly for longer than 30 minutes, or if you just appreciate hearing the nuances of a song. I was hesitant to buy them after reading about the condensation issue but I’ve worn them now for several weeks and have only seen condensation once, which came during a marathon writing session. The carrying case is a joke but if you’re spending this much for headphones, I suppose you know how to take care of nice things. Buy the headphones, you won’t regret them.

review_text: It's so disappointing to pay extra for the "newer" version of something only to find out the old version was better. This is exactly what happened with the gen 3 airpods. If you love your current airpods just hang on to them. It's getting really annoying that apple only tells you about the "new" features and doesn't tell you what you lose when you upgrade. First they changed the shape so it doesn't fit your ear as well as the prior version. I have to try to jam these in my ear and they constantly fall out. Be sure to try them on before you buy them. Secondly, they got rid of the super useful tap controls and replaced them with the less useful 'force' control. Which means instead of just tapping your airpod to pause or accept a call you have to feel around your whole head to find the tiny little magic spot on the tiny little stem of the airpod (which is in your ear now since it's shorter than before) and gently pleasure it until it does what apple wants it to (NOT what you want it to- see below). Why anyone would think squeezing the thing would be better than just a simple tap is beyond me. you have to use at least 2 fingers to gently message the stem in just the right way, rather than just a one finger tap (while you are also holding your keys or wallet for example). Last but not least if you are planning to customize the way you use them (the gestures), like you used to be able to do on the OLDER cheaper version you can forget it. That's gone. Can't customize the new ones. You just have to learn the 42 preset apple gestures of squeeze once for this, three times for that, now spin around and squeeze the other one for siri. It's maddening. I wore these for an hour then spent 3 hours trying to figure out how to adjust the gestures before i gave up. I immediately went back to my old airpods (gen2) which are far superior to the new more expensive ones. i will probably re-gift these to someone for xmas and I will definitely be betting another gen 2 set.

Perfect Fit.reason: The user mentions struggling to fit the new version, indicating a problem with perfect fit.

Wear Resistance.reason: The reviewer mentions using multiple fingers to adjust settings, indicating wear resistance issues.

review_text: Ok

I was kinda nervous getting something electronic on amazon but as always my experience was great. My first pair I received had a rattle sound in one pod. The return process was easy, my replacement pair arrived and they’ve been great.

I'm a specs guys. Found it really difficult to spend any money on something like this but the sound upgrade in quality is worth it. While the battery life is not as long as the previous versions, I never found myself out of battery from listening to headphones 24/7 except when in the office. In that case I had only had them in for noise cancelling. Funny story a sales manager told me to turn them down once so she could continue ranting and raving while talking over everyone around her. Now that I have these noise cancelling head phones and get to work from home everyday until at least 2022 (fingers crossed) this won't ever be a problem anymore. They are great for blocking out sound from bathroom toilets flushing when hard at work tho in an office setting I don't feel I should be subjected to sitting directly near the toilets anyway. What can I say, whether blocking out rude sales managers or toilet flushing these are the best. Great for gaming too, I find myself x10 better when focused and not even noticing loud stomping, yelling, or other peoples phone conversations when I have these in.

review_text: Long story short I lost my airpod pros. Saw these on major discount ($90 for Black Friday), and couldn't resist. So here's my take in four parts. Sound Quality. Noise Cancelling. Ambient pass through. Features. Sound quality -- versus the airpod pros, these are almost identical in sound. The only difference is the beats buds are a touch bass heavy. It's good, punchy bass, but noticeably more than the pros which were more balanced with its lows. So if you're a bass head, or hip-hop enthusiast, the beats win hands down. Noise cancelling -- now this could have to do with the shape of my ear, but I found the beats to actually be a step ABOVE in noise cancelling. My gf still has her airpods and I compared. The seal the beat creates, paired with its noise cancelling makes it seem like the world doesn't exist. Beats win for me in NC. Ambient pass-through -- this is where the beats lose out. Their noise cancelling is too good. So even when you have transparency turned on the world sound a little muddied out, while with the airpods, if I had transparency on it was almost like I wasn't wearing them. I miss that alot. So if you are in an environment where you need to hear what people are saying without removing your buds then airpods might be your pick. It's not to say that beats mak you deaf or anything but things are just noticeably quieter in transparance vs airpods with its full clarity. Features -- another loss for the beats. They don't work with the beats app so no equalizer function... why beats???? WHY?? So you'll have to go third party if you want to equalize. The beats also won't stop the music or video you're watching if one falls out. No biggy for me as I like that but some people might not. Other than that it has similar functionality to airpods. One click to pause, two to skip, etc. It doesn't auto switch between devices but does actually switch pretty similarly. Takes maybe two clicks to switch it to my mac. The pairing is a breeze with iphones. Just open the case next to your iphone and voila. It's paired.

review_text: I see people raving up and down about the perfection and wonderfulness of these headphones; I’m not as completely blown away by them as those people are. Quality of construction does seem to be excellent (although because Amazon doesn’t lower all prices at the start of Prime Day but only lowers some of them well after Prime Day has begun, I had to send back the first pair I bought and purchase another pair to get a discounted price, and the piston things for extending and compressing the size of the headphones was smoother on the first pair I had) and they’re comfortable enough to wear for extended periods, but while it may be a limitation of Bluetooth (I don’t know), I haven’t noticed myself being amazed by their bass response. No, in fact I just now watched the video for Shake It Off and listened to a couple of Lana Del Rey tracks and these headphones are definitely lacking in bass response – as in, there’s very little, and what there is gets completely lost under any midrange and high-end sounds that may be playing at the same time. They’re tinny-sounding. I have to wonder what the people are listening to with these who are praising the sound quality so enthusiastically. On the other hand, I don’t ordinarily listen to music anymore, but only to talks and lectures; While I’m watching movies is probably the only time I’m going to really want big bass, and I watched The Revenant last night and didn’t feel like I was missing anything… So their construction is much more solid than that of probably most other headphones on the market, they’re comfortable to wear, and there’s a nice ecosystem of accessories out there, but I personally find the sound quality quite lacking and I’m going to have to decide whether to hang onto these or return them.

review_text: I love my Airpods Pro 1 that I purchased in early 2020. They were worlds better than anything else I had tried and I have used them weekly ever since. The new version 2 looked interesting, but the old ones worked so well there wasn't any push to fix what wasn't broke. Until the Black Friday sale caught my eye and I decided to try them. In short: Wow. I'm in love again. I rather prefer the increased base, and the sound overall is brighter. The other features like the Location should be great to have. Overall, I don't regret this at all. I've fallen in love again.

I see lots of people around me using these all the time and I thought perhaps I was missing out on something, but truthfully, these are annoying and I'm always worried that I'll lose one, so I don't like wearing them outside my home. review_text: I tried low end earbuds ~$40 and I wasn’t happy with the muffled sound. However, the thought paying $200+ for earbuds too much. So, I bought Creative’s mid range units at $80. I’ve kept these as they were a big improvement over the cheap ones. They are hard to connect to my iPads and I didn’t think I was hearing all the jazz I was listening to. I finally gave in after reading all the rave reviews of Apples top AirPods. Well, they’re great, by a large factor. I can hear the umph in the music as well as background brush strokes on the symbols. I still wonder about Apple’s other, mid-level units which got high reviews...? However, these connect effortlessly to any Apple device they’re next to, AND without reading a manual. Further, the controls are minimal and you learn them automatically, on screen when you first connect. Overall, I like them well enough to keep even though I cringe at the price of these little used accessories =&

Amazing Sound Quality.reason: Strong positive statement about sound quality.

review_text: ...and at the end of the day that is all that really matters. She says they fit her well and she likes the sound quality. They are easy enough for her to use w/her iPhone or iPad. I caught these on sale, which Amazon does from time to time, so the cost was not too bad. The batteries last as long she needs them to last, so that is good. Even when they do need charging she can throw them on the case and put the case on a charging pad or plug it in. I'd recommend these ear buds.

review_text: Meh. I got my first AirPods recently. I was thrilled with them. So much better than corded headphones. I upgraded to these because they are supposed to be “better”. Not really. They are less comfortable. They fall out all the time. They are slimey and gross feelings when sweaty. The noise cancellation is real. It’s amazing how much it cuts out though I rarely want to be cut off like this. I can’t hear the doorbell (I literally missed being informed about a gas leak in my neighborhood because of this) and it’s dangerous on a bike. If you leave it open for ambient noise there’s a lot of crackling sounds from wind or it shifting in your ear. Also the mic seems poorer - or at least my family complains more they can’t understand me more often with these than the previous hard AirPods ( the ones without the silicone ear piece.)
